concept: Contaminant / Agent Present Outside a Stated Expected Condition
description: An agent present outside a stated expected condition. A contaminant may turn out harmless or even naturally occurring; the stronger claim of actual impairment belongs to 'pesenu' (pollute), a separate word for a separate finding.
sound_symbolism: Light 'hi' enters the narrow hiss and stressed round vowel of 'so' and preserves the complete sound of 'hiso' (clean). 'Phi' changes that settled ending into breath pressed between the lips and released toward bright 'i'. The word begins with a familiar clear condition, then its last syllable becomes newly audible in the air around it.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun: 'ha tinoa ha phialu hisophi nai.' (The dye is a contaminant in this water.) Name medium, expected condition, amount, source, and effect separately. Distinct from 'pesenu' (pollute), 'pekira' (toxic), 'whemori' (waste), and a source-specific analyte name.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Contamination is framed as a conditioned relation to a medium and expectation, not as an inherent essence of a substance or being.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The definition prevents technical contamination language from becoming a metaphor that dehumanizes outsiders, migrants, cultures, or disliked life.

tags.cognition: an agent classified relative to a stated expected condition
tags.nature: a material or organism investigated within an environmental medium
tags.physical: a substance, organism, or agent present in a sample, place, or process
